facts.text: Tottenville was renamed in 1869 after John Totten, whose family were Loyalists during the Revolutionary War.
The Conference House, built by Christopher Billop, hosted a 1776 peace negotiation attempt between British Admiral Howe and Continental Congress leaders.
The Conference House and surrounding parklands were restored and designated a National Historic Landmark in 1966, preserving over 260 acres of woodland and shoreline.
The Tottenville Historical Society, founded in 1984, curates artifacts, documents, and photographs that chronicle the neighborhood’s oldest settlement history.
Tottenville High School moved to a new campus in Huguenot in 1971, about three miles north of the original site.
The neighborhood sits at the confluence of Arthur Kill and Raritan Bay, directly across from Perth Amboy, New Jersey, where the Outerbridge Crossing spans the channel.
